description: Baseline capability for the Chancela desktop shell. Grants Tauri core defaults plus the FULL window-control permission set the custom (decorationless) title bar drives: drag, minimize, maximize/unmaximize/toggle-maximize, close, position/size, set-always-on-top, and the double-click-to-maximize the drag region performs; `opener:open-url` so external http(s) links open in the user's DEFAULT browser (allow-open-url grants the command, allow-default-urls grants the mailto/tel/http(s) URL scope open_url enforces); `process:allow-restart` so the crash screen's 'Reiniciar aplicação' action can relaunch the process (t26); and the export save bridge (`dialog:allow-save` + `fs:allow-write-file`) so generated files are written only after the user chooses a path in the native save dialog. This is our own trusted first-party UI. The `remote` clause is REQUIRED because the shell serves that UI from an in-process embedded server and navigates the main window to it at http://127.0.0.1:<ephemeral-port> (and, in `tauri dev`, http://localhost:5173) — a REMOTE origin as far as the ACL is concerned. Without it, every `plugin:window|*`, `plugin:opener|*`, `plugin:dialog|*`, `plugin:fs|*` and process IPC used here is rejected by the ACL. Both patterns are loopback-only (unreachable off-device); the granted file access is limited to the path scoped by the save dialog.
